content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 2, 1990


Gerald H. Upholt
Manager - Governmental Affairs
California Rifle and Pistol
Association, Inc.
555 Capitol Mall, Ste. 455
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-420

Dear Mr. Upholt:

You have requested advice on behalf of the California Rifle
and Pistol Association, Inc., Firearms Freedom Fund, Inc. doing
business as California Rifle and Pistol Association/Sportsmen's
Legislative Committee, and the California Rifle and Pistol
Association/Sportsmen's Legislative Committee Political Action
Committee concerning the campaign provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹ On July 19, 1990, I telephoned you for
additional information.

QUESTION

Is the California Rifle and Pistol Association/Sportsmen's
Legislative Committee Political Action Committee (the "Committee")
sponsored by California Rifle and Pistol Association, Inc. ("CRPA
Inc.") as well as Firearms Freedom Fund, Inc. (doing business as
California Rifle and Pistol Association/Sportsmen's Legislative
Committee)?

CONCLUSION

From the information provided in your letter and our
telephone conversation, it appears that CRPA Inc. qualifies as a
sponsor of the Committee.

1    Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Upholt, Gerald
Page 2


FACTS

CRPA Inc. is a statewide non-profit membership organization
formed for the purpose of promoting competitive marksmanship,
firearms education, training, safety, and other activities related
to lawful possession and use of firearms.

Firearms Freedom Fund, Inc. does business as and is publicly
known as California Rifle and Pistol Association/Sportsmen's
Legislative Committee. It was created by CRPA Inc. and it has an
eight member board of directors, all of whom are members of CRPA
Inc. It is our understanding that Firearms Freedom Fund, Inc. has
no general members of its own. The Committee's Statement of
Organization currently lists the California Rifle and Pistol
Association/Sportsmen's Legislative Committee as its sponsor.

Although CRPA Inc. does not solicit contributions to the
Committee, it publishes a monthly newsletter to the members and
the Committee is planning to place in the publication
advertisements soliciting contributions and articles describing
its activities. It is estimated that about 75% of the
contributions to the Committee will come from the membership of
CRPA Inc. In addition, CRPA Inc. may donate advertising space in
its publication to the Committee.

ANALYSIS

Section 82048.7(b) specifies that an entity sponsors a
committee if any of the following apply:

(1) The committee receives all or nearly all of
its contributions from the entity or its members,
officers, employees, or shareholders.

(2) The entity collects contributions for the
committee by use of payroll deductions or dues from
its members, officers, or employees.

(3) The entity alone or in combination with other
organizations, provides all or nearly all of the
administrative services for the committee.

(4) The entity, alone or in combination with other
organizations, sets the policies for soliciting
contributions or making expenditures of committee
funds.

Upholt, Gerald
Page 3


Because members of CPRA Inc. will provide nearly all of the
contributions to the Committee, it qualifies as a sponsor of the
Committee. Assuming that Firearms Freedom Fund, Inc. (dba
California Rifle and Pistol Association/Sportsmen's Legislative
Committee) also qualifies as a sponsor of the Committee under one
or more of the above criteria, the Committee may include in its
name a term that identifies both sponsors. You may wish to use
the name "California Rifle and Pistol Association Political Action
Committee."

Enclosed is a 1990 Statement of Organization (Form 410) you
may use to amend the information contained on your current
Statement of Organization. Please note that in Part IV, you
should provide the complete name and address of each sponsor.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
